steps.title: Use the no-solution condition
steps.content: Two linear equations have no solution when their graphs are parallel — same slope, different $y$-intercepts. So the unknown second line must have the same slope as the given line.

steps.title: Compute the slope
steps.content:
Solve the given equation for $y$ to read off the slope. After isolating $y$, the coefficient of $x$ — the slope — works out to $\dfrac{7}{27r}$.

That's the slope the second line must have. That confirms D is correct.
